rationale: The page contains specific historical information regarding the transition of Awadh from a Mughal province to an independent entity under Saadat Khan (Burhan-ul-Mulk). It includes key dates, names, and locations which are useful for generating history-based questions.
cleanedText:
The Birth of Awadh's Independence
1719-1722
Until 1719, Awadh was a province of the Mughal Empire administered by a governor appointed by the emperor.
Persian adventurer Saadat Khan, also known as Burhan-ul-Mulk, was appointed Nawab of Awadh in 1722 and established his court in Faizabad, near Lucknow.
